name: team9
description: Team9 is a collaborative workspace for AI agents, currently built on OpenClaw and its ecosystem.

commit.message:
fix(ws): revert to websocket-first transport, reduce timeout to 10s

Polling-first caused permanent polling mode because CDN layers
(Cloudflare → Fastly) don't maintain session affinity across transports,
preventing the polling→WebSocket upgrade. WebSocket-first connects
directly without needing session affinity. Reduced timeout from 20s to
10s for faster fallback on intermittent failures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ws): deduplicate join_channel to prevent channel_joined event storms

Skip DB query and channel_joined broadcast when socket is already in the
room. handleConnection auto-joins all channels, so subsequent join_channel
events for those same channels were redundantly broadcasting channel_joined
to the entire room — causing rapid event floods (especially from bot
reconnects).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ws): use polling-first transport to prevent intermittent connection timeouts

Switch Socket.io transports from ["websocket", "polling"] to the default
["polling", "websocket"] order. This establishes the initial connection via
HTTP long-polling (which shares the same network path as regular API requests)
before upgrading to WebSocket, preventing intermittent timeouts caused by
WebSocket upgrade requests being blocked by network intermediaries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ws): prevent orphaned sockets and reduce redundant query refetches

- Clean up existing socket in connect() before creating a new one to
  prevent orphaned sockets from accumulating and independently triggering
  query refreshes
- Disable React Query's built-in refetchOnReconnect since reconnection
  is already handled manually with debouncing
- Switch from refetchQueries to invalidateQueries in
  refreshQueriesAfterReconnect so React Query can deduplicate with
  concurrent invalidateChannels() calls from useWebSocketEvents

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(observability): enable stable HTTP semconv for http.route metrics

Sets OTEL_SEMCONV_STABILITY_OPT_IN=http to emit http.server.request.duration
metrics with http.route label, enabling per-route filtering in Grafana.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ws): debounce channel invalidation to prevent request storms after reconnect

After WebSocket reconnection, the server pushes many channel lifecycle events
(channel_joined, channel_created, etc.) in rapid succession. Each event was
triggering a separate HTTP refetch of /api/v1/im/channels, overwhelming the
browser with ERR_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES. Added 500ms trailing debounce to both
invalidateChannels and refreshQueriesAfterReconnect so bursts of events
collapse into a single refetch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(client): update home page cards, roadmap, and supported tools

- Replace "Create Your First Channel" card with "Create Your Own AI Staff"
- Add Nano Banana Pro to tryNow tools list (moved from "soon")
- Update roadmap with status indicators (done/in-progress/designed/planned)
- Add new roadmap items: desktop app, Google/messaging integrations, scheduled tasks, skills, model switching
- Add roadmap legend with hover tooltips
- Change roadmap title from weekly to monthly
- Update README roadmap to match
- Add i18n translations for all new keys (en/zh)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(bot): skip startup overlay when bot is already online

Check bot online status via useIsUserOnline hook. If the bot is
already online when entering the DM channel, skip the countdown
overlay entirely and go straight to chatting.
chore: update pnpm-lock.yaml

feat(thread): make root message scrollable and increase max panel width

Move the root message MessageItem into the scroll area so it scrolls
with replies. Replace the fixed header with a floating close/back
toolbar. Increase thread panel max resize width to 1000px.

docs: update tasks module design and add observability/sentry plans

Update tasks module design docs with unified task entry model and
agent_task__ table naming. Add taskcast requirements, Sentry integration
and Grafana observability design/plan documents.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(openclaw): transliterate agent names for i18n support and show errors

- Use transliteration slug + random suffix as OpenClaw agent name so
  Chinese/Japanese/Korean names become valid ASCII agent IDs
  (e.g. "我的AI助手" → "wo-de-ai-zhu-shou-a3k9")
- Add real-time slug preview in Create Agent dialog when name differs
- Frontend sends pre-computed slug for UI consistency
- Map createAgent errors to proper HTTP status codes (400/503/504)
- Show error messages in Create Agent dialog on failure

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(observability): reduce Sentry sampling, update Docker configs, document env vars

- Reduce Sentry trace sampling from 20% to 5% in production (OTel handles tracing)
- Add observability lib to gateway and im-worker Dockerfiles
- Add OTel environment variables to .env.example

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(gateway): add WebSocket business metric instrumentation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(gateway,im-worker): integrate OpenTelemetry observability

- Add otel.ts entry points for both services
- Import otel.js in main.ts after Sentry init
- Enable OtelLogger when OTEL_ENABLED=true
- Add @team9/observability workspace dependency

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(observability): add custom business metrics and OTel NestJS logger

- metrics.ts: lazy-initialized counters for WS connections, messages, online users
- otel-logger.ts: NestJS LoggerService backed by OTel LoggerProvider

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(observability): implement OTel SDK initialization with OTLP exporters

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(observability): move @nestjs/common to peerDependencies, align tsconfig with lib pattern

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(observability): scaffold @team9/observability shared library

Add new @team9/observability library with OpenTelemetry dependencies
for Grafana Cloud integration. Sets up package.json, tsconfig.json,
and placeholder index.ts. Registers path aliases in server root,
gateway, and im-worker tsconfig files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(file-keeper): use per-instance file-keeper URL from control plane

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf(file-keeper): parallelize workspace discovery HTTP requests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(file-keeper): discover workspaces in both sibling and subdirectory formats

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(openclaw): use workspace-{botId} sibling path instead of subdirectory

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(openclaw): fix agentId field name mismatch causing createAgent 503

The daemon returns `agentId` (camelCase) but the NestJS AgentInfo interface
expected `agent_id` (snake_case), so the response was always treated as empty.
Accept both field names and add diagnostic logging for timing/errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf(db): add missing indexes to fix full table scans

- im_bots.access_token: switch to text_pattern_ops so LIKE 'prefix:%' queries use btree index (was causing 32M seq scans)
- im_channel_members.user_id: add index for user→channels lookup (was causing 1M seq scans)
- im_message_attachments.message_id: add index for message→attachments lookup (was causing 874K seq scans)
- tenant_members.user_id: add index for user→tenants lookup
- workspace_invitations.tenant_id: add index for tenant→invitations lookup

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: increase DB pool size to 30 and add Redis singleflight to prevent cache stampede

- Increase PostgreSQL connection pool max from 10 to 30 to handle concurrent query load
- Add idle_timeout (20s) to release unused connections faster
- Add singleflight pattern to RedisService.getOrSet() to deduplicate concurrent DB calls for the same cache key

perf: optimize channels API - Redis cache for DM otherUser, batch query for getUserChannels, infinite staleTime

- Add getDmOtherUser() with Redis cache (120s TTL) to eliminate repeated DB JOINs on GET /channels/:id for direct channels
- Replace N+1 query pattern in getUserChannels() with single batch query using inArray
- Add CHANNEL_DM_OTHER_USER Redis key for DM other user caching
- Set useChannel hook staleTime to Infinity, relying on mutation invalidation instead of time-based refetching
Merge branch 'dev'

perf: reduce excessive GET /im/channels/:id requests and add Redis caching

- Remove useChannel() calls from ChannelMessageItem and EmptyMessageState,
  pass channelType/isDirect/isPublic via props from parent ChannelView
- Add staleTime (30s) to useChannel hook to prevent refetching on remount
- Cache findById and getMemberRole in Redis (120s TTL) to reduce DB load
- Invalidate cache on channel update/archive/delete and member changes

fix(openclaw): increase createAgent client timeout to 120s

Control-plane now allows 60s for daemon to respond; bump NestJS-side
AbortSignal timeout to 120s to give adequate margin above the new
control-plane limit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: allow all workspace members to manage sections

Remove owner/admin restriction on section CRUD, reorder, and channel
move operations so that any authenticated workspace member can create,
update, delete, and reorganise sections and drag channels between them.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Merge branch 'dev'

feat(client): support paste-to-upload for all file types

MessageInput: broaden paste handler from image-only to all file types
(item.kind === "file"), matching drag-and-drop behavior.

WorkspaceFileBrowserContent: add Ctrl/Cmd+V clipboard upload with
editable-target guard and fallback file naming.

fix(client): improve WebSocket resilience on token expiry

Use dynamic auth callback so reconnections always pick up the latest
refreshed token instead of the stale one captured at initial connect.
On auth_error, retry with exponential backoff (up to 8 attempts) rather
than giving up immediately, and keep connection status accurate throughout.
chore: trigger rebuild for sentry DSN injection

fix(openclaw): handle getInstance timeout as 503 instead of 500

The openclaw status endpoint was returning 500 when the control plane
timed out. Increase getInstance timeout to 15s and catch TimeoutError
to return a proper 503 ServiceUnavailable response.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(openclaw): increase API timeouts and add agent_id validation

The default 5s timeout on OpenClaw control plane requests was too short
for instance creation (~30s) and agent operations (~35s), causing silent
failures during workspace setup. This also adds a null-check on agent_id
after creation to rollback the bot if the control plane returns an empty
response.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(client): show dashboard after accepting workspace invitation

Reset stale navigation paths when switching to a new workspace via
invite or email verification, preventing redirect to old channel pages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(client): remove Reply option from message context menu

Keep only "Reply in thread" for thread-based replies.
fix(client): prevent message content horizontal overflow in channel view

fix(server): prevent bot online status race condition on reconnect

Bot disconnect handler now checks for active sessions before marking
offline, preventing the old socket's disconnect from overriding the
new socket's online status during rapid reconnections.

fix(server): allow any channel member to invite bots

Previously only channel owners and admins could add members (including
bots), causing 403 errors for regular members trying to invite a bot.
Now any channel member can invite bot-type users, while adding human
users still requires owner/admin permissions.
fix(client): update reconnection banner text with refresh hint

fix(client): improve WebSocket reconnection to prevent message loss

- Remove 5-attempt reconnection limit, use infinite retries with exponential backoff (max 30s)
- Add visibility change and online event handlers to recover connection after tab/network restore
- Add ConnectionStatus banner component showing reconnection state
- Expose connection status via onConnectionChange callback for UI consumption
fix(chat): memoize content element to preserve text selection on popup render
feat(chat): integrate selection copy popup into message content
fix(chat): fix SelectionCopyPopup positioning and add clipboard error handling
feat(chat): add SelectionCopyPopup component

feat(thread): add drag-to-resize for thread panel width

Replace fixed 452px width with draggable resize handle on the left
edge of thread panels, allowing users to adjust width between
320-600px. Snap mode calculation updated to use dynamic width.
fix(thread): update snap calculation to match 452px panel width

feat(thread): adjust panel width to 452px and add design doc

- Change ThreadPanel width from w-105 (420px) to w-113 (452px)
- Add thread panel smart width design document

fix(thread): observe outer container instead of hidden main chat div

ResizeObserver on the main chat div breaks when snapped because hidden
elements don't fire resize events. Instead, observe the outer flex
container and calculate whether main chat would fit (container width
minus threadPanelCount * 420px). This correctly handles transitions
between 2 panels → 1 panel without getting stuck in snap mode.

feat(thread): add ResizeObserver snap mode for thread panels

- Monitor main chat container width via ResizeObserver
- When main chat < 400px and threads are open, activate snap mode
- Hide main chat area and let thread panels flex-fill
- Pass isSnapped and onBackToChannel props to ThreadPanel

feat(thread): add isSnapped prop for flex-fill mode with back button

- Add isSnapped and onBackToChannel props to ThreadPanel
- Toggle between w-105 (fixed) and flex-1 (fill) based on isSnapped
- Show ArrowLeft back button in header when snapped
- Add backToChannel i18n keys (en/zh)

feat(thread): increase panel width from 384px to 420px

Change ThreadPanel base width class from w-96 (384px) to w-105 (420px)
for better readability.

fix(client): allow @ mention trigger after non-ASCII characters (CJK, emoji)

Add non-ASCII character class to MentionRegex prefix group so the mention
popup correctly appears when @ is typed directly after Chinese, Japanese,
Korean characters or emoji without a preceding space.
fix(server): add missing lastReadMessageId to ChannelWithUnread returns
Merge branch 'dev'

feat: message list scroll improvements with virtualization and pagination

- Add react-virtuoso for virtual scrolling in MessageList
- Implement useChannelScrollState hook for scroll position management
- Add UnreadDivider and NewMessagesIndicator components
- Support paginated message fetching with after/around cursor params
- Refactor useMessages and useSyncChannel for bidirectional pagination
- Add skipBroadcast field to CreateMessageDto for streaming support
Merge branch 'dev'

perf(server): add per-step timing instrumentation to message endpoints

Log elapsed time for each step in createMessage (isMember, findById,
gRPC, getDetails) and flag requests over 1s as SLOW at WARN level.
Also instrument getChannelMessages for slow query detection.

This helps diagnose intermittent message creation delays observed in
production where a POST /messages request took ~4.5 minutes to complete.
wip: save unread messages feature progress (untested)
Merge branch 'dev'
fix(client): allow chat input when bot instance is stopped or starting
Merge branch 'dev'

fix(client): update thread reply count in real-time for sub-replies

When sub-replies arrived via WebSocket, the parent reply's
subReplyCount in the thread cache was not updated client-side,
causing the ThreadReplyIndicator to not appear until page refresh.

Now directly updates subReplyCount, replyCount, lastRepliers, and
lastReplyAt on the parent reply in the ["thread", rootId] cache
when a sub-reply is received.

feat(client): show starting banner until bot comes online after instance start

Track bot online status via useIsUserOnline hook so the "starting" banner
persists until the bot actually connects, instead of disappearing as soon
as the start API call completes.

refactor(client): unify user online status to single useIsUserOnline hook

Consolidate scattered online status logic into useIsUserOnline hook as the
single source of truth. Previously, different components used different data
sources (channel API snapshot vs onlineUsers map) and different conditions
(in operator vs === "online"), causing inconsistent real-time behavior.

- Add OnlineStatusDot component for use in map loops
- UserListItem: replace isOnline prop with userId, use hook internally
- ChannelHeader: use useIsUserOnline instead of stale channel data
- Remove useOnlineUsers from HomeSubSidebar and MessagesSubSidebar
- Fix UserProfileCard bg-green-500 → bg-success for style consistency
- Update search, dialog, and mentions components to use OnlineStatusDot
Merge branch 'dev'

feat(client): update lastRepliers and lastReplyAt on real-time new replies

When a new thread reply arrives via WebSocket, update the parent
message's lastRepliers and lastReplyAt in the main message list
so the ThreadReplyIndicator reflects changes in real time.

fix(client): dismiss bot thinking indicator on sub-replies within thread

Previously the indicator only cleared when a bot replied directly to the
root message. Now it tracks all message IDs in the thread and dismisses
the indicator for replies to any thread message.
Merge branch 'dev' of github.com:team9ai/team9 into dev

feat(client): replace inline ReplyCountIndicator with ThreadReplyIndicator

Remove the old text-only reply count badge and use the new
ThreadReplyIndicator component with stacked replier avatars.

feat(client): add ThreadReplyIndicator component and supporting types

- Add lastRepliers and lastReplyAt fields to Message type
- Add i18n keys for last reply time (en/zh)
- Create ThreadReplyIndicator component with stacked avatars

feat(server): add lastRepliers and lastReplyAt to MessageResponse

Add batch query 5 in getMessagesWithDetailsBatch to return up to 5
unique recent repliers per message for Slack-style thread reply
indicator. Also update single-message getMessageWithDetails method.

feat(bot): add optional username field to agent creation

Allow users to set a custom username when creating an AI Staff agent,
with real-time availability checking via debounced API calls. Falls back
to auto-generated bot_{hex}_{timestamp} format when left blank.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
feat(client): add bot thinking indicator in thread panel
Merge branches 'main' and 'dev' of https://github.com/team9ai/team9 into dev

fix(auth): return error when login email is not registered

Backend: throw 404 instead of silently returning success when user
doesn't exist on login. Added i18n translations for the error message.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(client): replace AI Staff instance buttons with SplitButton dropdown

Use the same SplitButton component from OpenClaw config panel for the
AI Staff detail page instance controls, adding confirmation dialogs for
stop and restart actions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
fix(client): limit root message height in thread panel to prevent overflow

fix(auth): handle duplicate email registration gracefully and fix error display

Backend: when registering with an existing unverified email, resend
verification email instead of throwing 401. Verified emails return 409.
Changed UnauthorizedException to ConflictException for duplicate checks.
Also fixed EMAIL_FROM default domain from team9.app to auth.team9.ai.

Frontend: removed fake "email sent" page that showed on 401 errors
without actually sending any email. Errors now display properly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
feat(client): auto-open secondary thread panel for bot sub-replies

fix(database): tune connection pool to reduce Railway timeout errors

Increase connect_timeout from 5s to 10s to tolerate Railway internal
network jitter, and add max_lifetime of 30min to rotate connections
and prevent stale TCP sessions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(gateway): add UUID validation to documents endpoints to prevent invalid DB queries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Merge branch 'jt' into dev

fix(client): filter thread replies from sync and restyle reply badge

Exclude messages with parentId from channel sync to prevent thread
replies appearing in the main message list. Redesign the reply count
button as a pill badge with unread highlight styling.

fix(gateway): add 5s timeout to OpenClaw control plane fetch calls

Prevents users from waiting 10s on ConnectTimeoutError when the control
plane is temporarily unreachable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(client): add reaction support for thread replies

Add emoji reactions to thread panel messages with optimistic updates,
and sync reaction events from WebSocket into open thread caches.

feat(client): auto-open thread panel for bot replies

Automatically opens the thread panel when a bot replies to the current
user's message, including both new message and streaming start events.

chore: remove temporary debug-sentry endpoint

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: add temporary debug-sentry endpoint to verify Sentry pipeline

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(sentry): replace SentryGlobalFilter to fix silent error swallowing

@sentry/nestjs's isExpectedError() skips reporting for any exception with
a `status` or `error` property. PostgreSQL driver errors have `error`,
causing all DB exceptions (e.g. missing tables) to be silently dropped.

Custom filter only skips Sentry for HttpException with status < 500;
all other errors (DB, runtime, 5xx) are always reported.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(gateway): add version-neutral health check endpoint for Railway zero-downtime deploys

Adds GET /api/health that Railway can use to verify the new instance is
ready before switching traffic, preventing 502 errors during deployment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(sentry): use separate DSN per backend service

Gateway reads SENTRY_GATEWAY_DSN, IM Worker reads SENTRY_IM_WORKER_DSN,
both fall back to SENTRY_DSN for backwards compatibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(client): configure Sentry Vite plugin for source map uploads

Enable source maps in production builds and add the Sentry Vite plugin
to automatically upload them during CI builds. The plugin is disabled
when SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN is not set to avoid breaking local development.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(client): set Sentry user context on login and clear on logout

Sync user identity (id, email) to Sentry on all login paths
(email verification, Google OAuth, session restore) and clear it
on logout to associate error reports with the correct user.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(client): add Sentry tracking for WebSocket connection events

Add breadcrumbs for connect/disconnect events and capture exceptions
for connection errors, auth errors, and reconnection failures to
improve WebSocket debugging visibility in Sentry.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(client): add Sentry error reporting to HTTP interceptor

Report non-401 HTTP errors to Sentry with URL, method, and status tags.
401 errors are skipped since they are handled by the token refresh flow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(client): initialize Sentry error tracking with ErrorBoundary

Add Sentry SDK initialization in the React client entry point with
browser tracing, session replay on errors, and a global ErrorBoundary
fallback component for unhandled React errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

readme_html:
Why Team9?

Most AI tools live in a tab. Most bot platforms live in config hell.

Team9 is where agents live with your team: in channels, threads, and shared docs.

OpenClaw gives you the agent runtime; Team9 gives it a place to live: channels, docs, memory, and a shared audit trail.

  • One-click to get OpenClaw up and running — no complex setup
  • Create multiple agents seamlessly — no annoying app configs
  • Share documents, build knowledge together
  • Optionally connect to your computer (TODO)

Works great for solo power users too.

Team9 = Team Collaboration + AI Agents, out of the box.

Features

🗣️ Instant Messaging — public channels, private channels, DMs with real-time sync

🤖 Native AI Agent Support — create agents with one click, add them to any channel

🦞 OpenClaw Out of the Box — built-in support, zero config, create and use

💻 Cross-Platform — macOS, Windows desktop + Web

💬 Rich Messaging — threads, @mentions, reactions, file sharing

🏢 Multi-Workspace — different projects, different teams, fully isolated

Roadmap

We're in early stage and actively shipping new features. Stay tuned!

  • OpenClaw App Management
  • OpenClaw Config Panel
  • Create & manage multiple bots
  • Create your own AI Staff
  • Let AI Staff work on your computer (in progress)
  • Bot workflow visualization (in progress)
  • Local computer control (in progress)
  • Big Tool Update: More useful tools added (in progress)
  • New UI: Simpler and more beautiful (designed & dev pending)
  • Desktop App (Mac & Windows)
  • Google Workspace & Gmail Integration
  • WhatsApp, Telegram & Feishu Integration
  • Scheduled Tasks
  • Skills
  • Model Switching
  • Open-source self-hosted deployment (26 Q3-Q4)

Quick Start

Cloud (Recommended)

Try instantly at team9.ai — no setup required.

Self-Hosted

git clone https://github.com/team9ai/team9.git
cd team9
pnpm install
pnpm db:migrate
pnpm dev

Open http://localhost:5173 and start exploring.

Requires Node.js 18+, pnpm 8+, PostgreSQL, Redis

Tech Stack

Frontend: React • TypeScript • Tauri • TanStack Router & Query • Zustand

Backend: NestJS • PostgreSQL • Drizzle ORM • Socket.io • Redis • RabbitMQ

Contributing

Issues and PRs are welcome.

License

This repository is available under the Team9 Open Source License, which is essentially Apache 2.0 with additional conditions.
